verses.1: “Surely there is a mine for silver,
verses.2: Iron is taken out of the earth,
verses.3: Man sets an end to darkness,
verses.4: He breaks open a shaft away from where people live.
verses.5: As for the earth, out of it comes bread.
verses.7: That path no bird of prey knows,
verses.8: The proud animals have not trodden it,
verses.9: He puts his hand on the flinty rock,
verses.10: He cuts out channels among the rocks.
verses.11: He binds the streams that they don’t trickle.
verses.14: The deep says, ‘It isn’t in me.’
verses.15: It can’t be gotten for gold,
verses.16: It can’t be valued with the gold of Ophir,
verses.17: Gold and glass can’t equal it,
verses.18: No mention will be made of coral or of crystal.
verses.19: The topaz of Ethiopia will not equal it.
verses.20: Where then does wisdom come from?
verses.21: Seeing it is hidden from the eyes of all living,
verses.24: For he looks to the ends of the earth,
verses.25: He establishes the force of the wind.
verses.26: When he made a decree for the rain,
verses.27: then he saw it, and declared it.
